_comment: THE single machine-readable source of truth for Mental's supported platform matrix. Every consumer — core/build.gradle.kts task registration + plugin.yml api-version, scripts/integration-matrix.sh, .github/workflows/{build,release}.yml, the release-notes range — reads this file (jq / JsonSlurper). No Minecraft version or JDK literal may live anywhere else. Entries are version-sorted ascending, so entries[0] is the support floor (the legacy backport floor 1.9.4) and entries[-1] is the ceiling — the release-notes range and floor derivations read positionally. Paper entries drive the full era suites; the folia entry (platform: folia, suites: combat-smoke) boots a real Folia server and runs the boot suite + the Folia combat smoke. suites = full | boot | combat-smoke; a 'boot' entry runs only the classload/boot-safety suite. The Java-version-bypass server-flag story is DEAD: Mental ships a Multi-Release mega-jar whose base tree is class-v52 (Java 8), so every legacy Paper build now runs on the NEWEST Java it boots FLAGLESS — no outdated-Java advisories, maximal JVM performance (GC/JIT gains). 1.9.4–1.12.2 have no Java version guard and boot even on Java 25, but Java 24+ prints the JEP-472 native-access / sun.misc.Unsafe deprecation warning walls, so their newest warning-free rung is Java 21. The hard-cap builds run at their measured newest-clean-flagless rung (probed live 2026-07-03): 1.13.2 → 13 (14 and 15 both refuse with 'Only up to Java 12'), 1.15.2 → 14 (its guard names 14 as the cap), 1.16.5 → 16 (its own JvmChecker asks for exactly 16). bytecodeTier = the Multi-Release tier the entry's plugin loader × JVM ACTUALLY reads out of the mega jar, asserted live by the tester (-Dmental.tester.tier) so which tree loaded is a per-version FACT, never an assumption. The rule is simple once measured live (2026-07-03): the mega jar carries exactly two trees — base v52 and versions/17 (major 61) — and a JVM selects versions/17 only when its own feature version is ≥ 17 (the MRJAR spec) AND the plugin loader honors runtime-versioning. So bytecodeTier follows the entry's JVM: 1.9.4–1.12.2 have no Java cap and run on Java 21, whose ≥17 runtime reaches versions/17 through their URLClassLoader-delegating plugin loaders → major 61 (the round-2 javap map mis-placed the boundary at 1.12.2; live boots prove 1.12.2's loader honors MR exactly like 1.9.4–1.11.2); 1.13.2 (Java 13), 1.15.2 (Java 14) and 1.16.5 (Java 16) are hard-capped BELOW Java 17, so versions/17 is out of reach for their runtimes and they read the base tree (major 52) whatever their loader does; every 1.17.1+ paper and the folia entry runs Java 17+ and reads versions/17 (major 61) — byte-identical to the 2.3.x line. NOTE: 1.14.4 was long the one KNOWN HOLE in the range purely because its terminal Paper build hard-caps at Java 13 with no IgnoreJavaVersion escape, so the OLD Java-17-classfile jar could not load on ANY JVM 1.14.4 accepts (see docs/superpowers/research/2026-07-02-legacy-boot-viability.md). The mega jar closes it: its v52 base tree classloads on every Java 8–13 JVM 1.14.4 accepts, so 1.14.4 is now a FULL-tier entry running at its Java-13 cap (13 boots 'Done' clean flagless; 14 and 15 both refuse with 'Only up to Java 12') and reads the base tree (major 52) since Java 13 < 17 keeps versions/17 out of reach — see docs/superpowers/research/2026-07-03-v1_14_R1-shapes.md.
